hypothesis: The current generic /work/xr candidate may still hide a kernel or responsive regression; fresh falsification should reject promotion if XR no longer holds zero critical and zero major diffs while preserving IMC flagship isolation.
summary: Rejected: fresh XR falsification still fails the phase acceptance rule with `14` critical layout diffs; IMC flagship invariants remain intact and XR responsive overflow is clear.

notes: `deterministic-design-system/reports/responsive/responsive.verification.md` shows `/work/xr` has no horizontal overflow on laptop, tablet, or mobile.

quality_findings_summary: `npm run audit:quality -- --baseUrl=http://127.0.0.1:3016` produced `1` critical, `2` major, and `3` minor findings across the audited shared routes.
recommendation: Do not promote this C4 candidate. Repair the XR live/reference geometry mismatch in the shared work-lane surface and clear the shared quality findings before rerunning falsification.
next_hypothesis: IMC flagship isolation is still intact, but the shared work-lane rendering used by `/work/xr` has regressed away from the locked reference geometry; the next bounded slice should identify which current kernel/layout change introduced the global XR y/x/width drift.
learning_captured: Fresh falsification was necessary because the repo-local XR artifacts did not match the cycle summary.
Current `/work/xr` failure is a layout-law regression, not a responsive overflow failure.
The flagship `/imc` route still preserves `imc.*` measurement keys and avoids `work.lane.*` page-level keys in rendered output.
commands_run: pwd && git rev-parse --short HEAD && git status --short && rg --files deterministic-design-system/reports deterministic-design-system/maps site/src/scripts site/src/lib/layout | sed -n '1,120p'

node --input-type=module -e "const res=await fetch('http://127.0.0.1:3016/imc'); const html=await res.text(); const hasImc=html.includes('data-spec=\"imc.page\"'); const hasWorkLane=html.includes('data-spec=\"work.lane.page\"'); console.log(JSON.stringify({hasImc,hasWorkLane}, null, 2));"

node --input-type=module -e "const fs=require('node:fs'); const p='deterministic-design-system/maps/diff/work-xr.diff.json'; const data=JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(p,'utf8')); const counts=data.diffs.reduce((acc,d)=>{acc[d.severity]=(acc[d.severity]||0)+1; return acc;},{}); console.log(JSON.stringify({route:data.route, counts, top:data.diffs.sort((a,b)=>b.distance-a.distance).slice(0,5)}, null, 2));"

node --input-type=module -e "import fs from 'node:fs'; const p='deterministic-design-system/maps/diff/work-xr.diff.json'; const data=JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(p,'utf8')); const counts=data.diffs.reduce((acc,d)=>{acc[d.severity]=(acc[d.severity]||0)+1; return acc;},{}); const top=[...data.diffs].sort((a,b)=>b.distance-a.distance).slice(0,5); console.log(JSON.stringify({route:data.route, counts, top}, null, 2));"

node --input-type=module -e "import fs from 'node:fs'; const file='deterministic-design-system/reports/quality/quality.verification.json'; const data=JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(file,'utf8')); const counts=data.findings.reduce((acc,f)=>{acc[f.severity]=(acc[f.severity]||0)+1; return acc;},{}); console.log(JSON.stringify({counts, findings:data.findings}, null, 2));"
git rev-parse HEAD && git merge-base --is-ancestor 49ecb04bdeb6ba4803bf753d0d27c59260320cf0 HEAD; echo $?

git add 'deterministic-design-system/maps/diff/work-xr.diff.json' 'deterministic-design-system/maps/live/work-xr.live.json' 'deterministic-design-system/reports/quality/quality.verification.json' 'deterministic-design-system/reports/quality/quality.verification.md' 'deterministic-design-system/reports/responsive/responsive.verification.md' 'deterministic-design-system/reports/C4-20260403T165341Z-03.xr-falsification.rejected.md' && git commit -m "systems-qa: reject xr candidate"
known_risks: The fresh audit outputs contradict the brief's `runtime_focus.latest_audits` pass summary, so another lane may still be carrying a stale green state.
Shared quality audit remains red outside the target route (`home` missing a main landmark; duplicate title and description across routes), which broadens promotion risk beyond XR layout drift.
